LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CREATE A DUTY OF CARE OWED TO HIM UNDER BIAKANJA V. IRVING (1958) 49 CAL.2D 647, 650 SINCE THE ASSIGNMENT COVERED DETERMINING HIS PERSONAL INTEREST. THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN AN ACCOUNTANT AND CLIENT IS ORDINARILY NOT A FIDUCIARY ONE. "A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP IS CREATED WHERE A PERSON REPOSES TRUST AND CONFIDENCE IN ANOTHER AND THE PERSON IN WHOM SUCH CONFIDENCE IS REPOSED OBTAINS CONTROL OVER THE OTHER PERSON'S AFFAIRS." LYNCH V. CRUTTENDEN & CO. (1993) 18 CAL.APP.4TH 802, 809. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS SHOWING HOW DEFENDANT OBTAINED CONTROL OVER PLAINTIFF'S AFFAIRS OR OTHERWISE BECAME A FIDUCIARY. THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT HE IS AN INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE ORAL AGREEMENT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD/CONCEALMENT CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT HE RELIED ON DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED MISCONDUCT TO HIS DETRIMENT AND WAS DAMAGED BY THAT RELIANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON THE DEMURRER AS TO CLAIMS THAT COULD SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED IN LITIGATING THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD A STATUTORY, LEGAL, OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR SUCH AN AWARD. THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY TO ALLOW RECOVERY OF SUCH FEES IN THIS CASE. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTING TO ESTABLISH A RIGHT TO RECOVER OTHER ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED ELSEWHERE AS PART OF A SUBSTANTIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the professional negligence cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to create a duty of care owed to him under Biakanja v. Irving (1958) 49 Cal.2d 647, 650 since the assignment covered determining his personal interest. The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The relationship between an accountant and client is ordinarily not a fiduciary one. "A fiduciary relationship is created where a person reposes trust and confidence in another and the person in whom such confidence is reposed obtains control over the other person's affairs." Lynch v. Cruttenden & Co. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 802, 809. Plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts showing how defendant obtained control over plaintiff's affairs or otherwise became a fiduciary. The demurrer to the breach of oral contract cause of action is overruled because plaintiff sufficiently alleges that he is an intended third party beneficiary of the oral agreement. The demurrer to the fraud/concealment cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing that he relied on defendant's alleged misconduct to his detriment and was damaged by that reliance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the claim for punitive damages in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer as to claims that could support punitive damages. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for attorney's fees incurred in litigating this action. Plaintiff fails to plead a statutory, legal, or contractual basis for such an award. The tort of another doctrine does not apply to allow recovery of such fees in this case. This ruling is without prejudice to Plaintiff attempting to establish a right to recover other attorney's fees incurred elsewhere as part of a substantive damages claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
